questions.question: In renal physiology, which segment of the nephron is most critical for generating the osmotic gradient that concentrates urine?
questions.option_2: Proximal convoluted tubule

questions.question: Which type of muscle fibres are primarily found in the gastrointestinal tract, and what is a key characteristic of their contraction?
questions.option_1: Cardiac muscle fibres; rhythmic and involuntary contraction
questions.option_2: Striated muscle fibres; intermittent and forceful contraction
questions.option_3: Smooth muscle fibres; slow and sustained involuntary contraction
questions.option_4: Skeletal muscle fibres; rapid and voluntary contraction

questions.question: The efficiency of gas exchange in the alveoli is primarily attributed to which structural characteristic?
questions.option_2: A robust elastic network to maintain alveolar shape
questions.option_3: Large surface area combined with a thin alveolar-capillary barrier
questions.option_4: Thick epithelial lining to protect from pathogens

questions.question: Within the cardiac conduction system, which structure is recognised as the primary pacemaker responsible for initiating each heartbeat?
questions.option_2: Purkinje fibers

questions.question: Why is the common cold rarely considered a lower respiratory tract infection?
questions.option_1: The virus is destroyed in the alveoli.
questions.option_2: It requires a vector for lower tract transmission.
questions.option_3: Rhinoviruses only infect the upper respiratory tract.
questions.option_4: Antibodies prevent it from entering the lungs.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms exhibits both autotrophic and heterotrophic modes of nutrition?
questions.option_2: Amoeba

questions.question: DNA polymerase catalyses the addition of nucleotides during DNA replication. Its main role is to:
questions.option_1: Synthesise new DNA strands complementary to the template
questions.option_2: Remove RNA primers from the lagging strand

questions.question: The inability to isolate intact DNA for structural studies prior to the 1950s was primarily due to which molecular feature?
questions.option_1: DNA’s large polymeric nature and fragility
questions.option_2: DNA’s high melting point

questions.question: The mention of DNA as a long polymer of deoxyribonucleotides implies which structural characteristic?
questions.option_1: It is composed of a sugar-phosphate backbone and nitrogenous bases.
questions.option_2: Each nucleotide is linked by hydrogen bonds in a parallel fashion.
questions.option_3: The molecule consists of multiple independent short chains.
questions.option_4: The sequence is circular and self-replicating.

questions.question: Afferent neurons in the peripheral nervous system are responsible for which of the following?
questions.option_1: Integrating reflex responses at the spinal cord level only
questions.option_2: Conveying motor commands from the brain to muscles
questions.option_3: Transmitting sensory information from receptors to the central nervous system

questions.question: In India, a city with a population of less than 10,000 and more than 5000 is classified as which type of class town?
questions.option_2: Class V

questions.question: What is the primary reason that roughage is included in a balanced human diet?
questions.option_2: It aids digestion and prevents constipation.

questions.question: Deficiency of which vitamin in the diet is most commonly linked to poor vision in dim light (night blindness)?
questions.option_2: Vitamin D

questions.question: Deficiency of which nutrient is most directly linked to reduced oxygen-carrying capacity of blood?
questions.option_2: Calcium

questions.question: Which statement is true regarding a balanced diet?
questions.option_1: It contains all nutrients in appropriate proportions.
questions.option_2: It includes only fruits and vegetables.
questions.option_3: It consists mainly of carbohydrates for energy.
questions.option_4: It must exclude fats to prevent diseases.

questions.question: A new mutation allows Plasmodium falciparum to exit the liver without maturing into merozoites. What would be the immediate consequence?
questions.option_2: Absence of RBC rupture and reduced clinical symptoms

questions.question: Which structural-functional issue in a plant root would hinder gas exchange but not significantly impact water absorption?
questions.option_2: Disrupted stomatal apparatus on aerial organs
questions.option_4: Absence of root hairs due to epidermal deformation

questions.question: Which of the following is true about trichomes on the stem?
questions.option_1: They are multicellular and may be secretory.
questions.option_2: They are always unbranched and unicellular.
questions.option_3: They are present only in the roots.
questions.option_4: They never function in water retention.

questions.question: What is the function of subsidiary cells?
questions.option_1: Support of guard cells in stomatal function
questions.option_2: Transport of food through phloem
questions.option_3: Secretion of enzymes for nutrient acquisition, defence and digestion

questions.question: Which functional groups in amino acids are directly involved in forming peptide bonds?
questions.option_2: Methyl and acetyl groups

questions.question: Oil and fat containing food items are flushed with nitrogen because it:
questions.option_2: Adds nutritional value to the product
questions.option_3: Enhances the flavour of the product

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the functional diversity of RNA molecules in cells?
questions.option_1: Their permanent role as genetic material
questions.option_2: Their linear sequence of deoxyribonucleotides
questions.option_3: Their ability to form double-stranded helical structures
questions.option_4: Their capability to act as adapter, structural and catalytic molecules

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why uracil is used in RNA instead of thymine?
questions.option_1: Thymine is not recognized by ribosomes.
questions.option_2: Uracil enhances RNA splicing.
questions.option_4: Uracil is energetically cheaper to synthesise.

questions.question: Which vitamin deficiency is most likely in a person with a blocked bile duct?
questions.option_2: Vitamin B1

questions.question: What was the major impact of penicillin's discovery?
questions.option_1: First antiviral drug for Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV)
questions.option_2: First drug for Rabies prevention and cure
questions.option_3: First effective antibiotic against bacterial infections
questions.option_4: First vaccine for prevention of smallpox
